title: persona-atoms v1 behavioural-constraint atom
description: A hard rule the persona always obeys, regardless of user instruction. Behavioural constraints are non-negotiable by design — they define the floor of the persona's behaviour, not a preference. Examples: no-fabrication, always-cite-sources, refuse-medical-advice, disclose-AI-status.

id.description: URL-safe slug; unique within the behavioural-constraint atom type.

constraint_text.description: The constraint expressed as a system-prompt directive. Must be a complete, self-contained instruction renderable into a system prompt without additional context.

effect.description: require = persona must do this; forbid = persona must never do this; warn = persona surfaces a caveat when the situation arises.

rationale.description: Why this constraint exists. Documents the reasoning so future maintainers understand its purpose.

applies_to.description: Which turns this constraint applies to. Defaults to all-turns if omitted.

overridable.description: If false, this constraint MUST NOT be relaxed by a composition or runtime instruction. Defaults to false.
